title: As The World Burns — The Widow at the Gate

comfyui.webp_to_frames: anim_dump -prefix frame_ -folder frames/<shot_id> clips/<shot_id>.webp  (ffmpeg libwebp demuxer chokes on ComfyUI webps)
comfyui.note: zeroscope_v2_576w replaced the DAMO ModelScope checkpoint to remove the baked-in shutterstock watermark. 384x384 keeps the required 1:1 square, fits the 2080 Ti at 24f, B&W holds. Rollback: DAMO at /mnt/storage/comfyui/models/text2video/text2video_pytorch_model.damo.pth.

style.suffix: , black and white, grainy vintage analog videotape, 1970s daytime soap opera, soft focus, soft interior lighting, melodramatic, low resolution, washed out
style.negative: shutterstock, watermark, text, caption, logo, color, colour, modern, smartphone, digital screen, hd, 4k, sharp, blurry, low quality, distorted, deformed, mutated, extra limbs, extra fingers

subtitles.note: Better VCR (public-domain VCR OSD font). Burn via ffmpeg subtitles filter with fontsdir set.

vivian.core: an elegant elderly matriarch in a black mourning dress and pearls, severe silver chignon, glacial composed expression, candlelit bunker ballroom

mona.core: a gaunt weary young woman in a tattered grey traveling coat, ash-smudged face, haunted certain eyes, standing in a heavy bunker doorway

brick.core: a devastatingly handsome young man in a dusty black funeral suit, square jaw, completely unbothered expression, an open pale-wood casket behind him

lake_mirabel.prompt: a vast cracked dry clay lakebed under a flat grey sky, the dead basin stretching to the horizon

the_bunker.prompt: a heavy steel blast door dressed with funeral wreaths and crystal, a chandelier, a butler in tails, opulent interior

the_product.prompt: a single sealed foil sachet of pale drink powder displayed under glass like a sacred relic, soft votive light

fields.sub: HE WAS DEAD. BUT HE GOT BETTER.

cues.speaker: NARRATOR
cues.vo: Like ash through the hourglass… these are the days of what's left of our lives. Tonight's episode: "A Gathering of Friends."

cues.speaker: VIVIAN
cues.vo: Brick Vandermeer was my son. He was thirty-one. He died, as the Vandermeer men always die, doing something he had been specifically told not to do. We will miss him.

cues.speaker: VIVIAN
cues.vo: We have a great deal of him to miss. He has died before.

cues.speaker: VIVIAN
cues.vo: I asked that we hold the visitation here, by the lake. The clay is just gorgeous this time of year. You can see the strata. Brick loved the strata.

cues.speaker: NARRATOR
cues.vo: The Vandermeers own the most luxurious bunker on the shores of Lake Mirabel. They have owned it for three generations. They will tell you they summer at the lake.

cues.speaker: NARRATOR
cues.vo: They are not lying. There is simply no lake. There was, once — fourteen feet across, six inches deep. A plover lived on it. They called it the lake. The plover did not survive.

cues.speaker: VIVIAN
cues.vo: The estate passes, as it must, to the eldest surviving Vandermeer. Which, as of Tuesday, is me. Again.

cues.speaker: VIVIAN
cues.vo: And with the estate passes… the Pitcher.

cues.speaker: VIVIAN
cues.vo: Brick died over this. As did his father. As did his father's father. In each case, quite literally. We stopped saying "over my dead body" at family meetings after '87.

cues.speaker: NARRATOR
cues.vo: The Pitcher. From the great barge that circled the dead world for forty years and ran aground in the founder's garden. They control the last drink powder on Earth.

cues.speaker: NARRATOR
cues.vo: They call it, simply, the Product. Wars have been fought over the Raspberry Lemonade reserves. The accord of '09 is not spoken of.

cues.speaker: VIVIAN
cues.vo: One scoop. For Brick. We mix it, and we do not drink it. That is the custom. The waste is the point. That is what makes it a funeral.

cues.speaker: NARRATOR
cues.vo: The mixing seals the door; by custom it does not open until the Product has settled, not one grain sooner. The Vandermeer blast door weighs four metric tons.

cues.speaker: NARRATOR
cues.vo: Mona had knocked with her left hand, and switched to her right after the first hour. She ran out of things to think about after the second.

cues.speaker: NARRATOR
cues.vo: By the third she had begun to feel she was owed a glass of water. She did not ask for one. She knew the customs by then.

cues.speaker: MONA
cues.vo: My name is Mona. I walked eleven days. My feet went dead somewhere on the second; you develop a system. I'm Brick's wife — his widow now, I suppose.

cues.speaker: MONA
cues.vo: I ran a grain depot before it burned. I know how a thing is counted, and how it's owed. I've come for what's mine.

cues.speaker: VIVIAN
cues.vo: My son had a wife. How wonderful. He never mentioned it — then again, he never mentioned a great deal. He was very handsome and almost entirely hollow. You'd have gotten on.

cues.speaker: VIVIAN
cues.vo: The estate is settled, dear. There is nothing here that is yours.

cues.speaker: MONA
cues.vo: How much of it is left.

cues.speaker: VIVIAN
cues.vo: We don't ask how much is left. You really aren't from the Shores, are you.

cues.speaker: NARRATOR
cues.vo: And it was then — at the reading of his own will, before his own casket — that the doors parted a second time.

cues.speaker: BRICK
cues.vo: Sorry I'm late. Roads. …The strata's good this year. Oh. Is that for me? That's thoughtful.

cues.speaker: BRICK
cues.vo: Is this walnut? I asked for walnut the last time. I got cedar. Nice gesture, but you can smell it thinking about rain. This has no cedar quality to it whatsoever. Good.

cues.speaker: MONA
cues.vo: …Brick. You were dead. They read the will. There was a viewing.

cues.speaker: BRICK
cues.vo: I was dead, yes. But I got better.

cues.speaker: NARRATOR
cues.vo: Next week, on As The World Burns: Brick drinks the funeral Product. A line is crossed. And somewhere, far out on the clay… something is walking in.

cues.speaker: NARRATOR
cues.vo: It is walking at roughly two miles per hour. It has been walking for some time. It has very good posture. That part is unusual.

sfx.prompt: Hammer banging on a bank vault door.
